summary: Docker-based reproduction demonstrating SSRF via unvalidated image URL fetching. The PoC creates an internal HTTP server and shows that the PDF generator fetches this internal URL when processing a malicious survey JSON.
expected_security_impact: The PDF generator should fetch the internal URL (http://localhost:31337/internal-metadata) which simulates access to cloud metadata or internal services that should be blocked.
actual_observed_result: PoC bundle created with all necessary components. Docker build and execution requires significant time to set up Node.js environment, install dependencies, and compile the survey-pdf library.
verification_method: The internal server logs incoming requests. Successful SSRF is confirmed when the server receives a request from the PDF generator process, proving that arbitrary URLs can be fetched.
evidence_produced_by_run: PoC files created: Dockerfile, run.sh, exploit.js, server.js, malicious_survey.json
Vulnerable code path traced from survey JSON to fetch() call
Internal server code ready to receive and log SSRF requests
limitations_or_blockers: Docker build timeout during execution - environment setup requires extensive time for npm install and library compilation
Full verification requires complete Docker environment build and execution which exceeds reasonable time constraints for initial validation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
